commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a helper to detect version-mismatched path install records pointing at packaged bundled plugin output and wires it into post-core update convergence plus doctor registry repair with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, not via a live run: current main preserves path install records while existing bundled-shadow cleanup only covers managed npm/clawhub-style records, so the stale compiled path shadow is credible from code inspection.
commands.review_followup: Human review should wait on contributor real behavior proof and consciously accept the local compiled-path cleanup compatibility boundary.

target.title: fix(update): prune stale local bundled plugin shadows
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a version-aware stale local bundled-plugin install-record detector for official bundled plugin IDs.
- Prune those stale records during post-core update convergence before repair/smoke checks run.
- Teach `openclaw doctor --fix` to warn on and remove stale local bundled install-record shadows while preserving current, same-version, versionless, source-checkout, and arbitrary local path records.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/stale-local-bundled-plugin-install-records.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Real behavior proof
- Post-core convergence now drops a stale `discord` path record pointing at an old `dist/extensions/discord` output before handing baseline records to plugin repair.
- Doctor repair now rebuilds the registry with stale local bundled install records removed, so bundled plugin schemas from the current host win.
- The helper keeps non-stale local overrides: current bundled paths, same-version compiled paths, versionless compiled paths, source-checkout `extensions/<id>` paths, and arbitrary local plugin paths are not pruned.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
